Title: Create Written Incident Response Program (Reg S-P)
Detailed Description: CRITICAL (Phase 1, 0-30 days). AFFG's BCP has no written incident response program for data breaches or unauthorized access to customer information. Create a standalone Incident Response Plan or integrated IRP section covering detection procedures, containment protocols, forensic investigation steps, escalation matrix, evidence preservation, and recovery procedures, as required by the 2024 Reg S-P amendments.

Title: Establish Customer Breach Notification Procedures (Reg S-P)
Detailed Description: CRITICAL (Phase 1, 0-30 days). No procedures exist to notify affected individuals within 30 days of determining sensitive customer information was, or is reasonably likely to have been, accessed without authorization. Add a Breach Notification section: criteria for when notification is required, the 30-day timeline, notification-letter content requirements, the process to determine scope of affected individuals, and template notification letters.

Title: Establish Service Provider Oversight Framework (Reg S-P)
Detailed Description: CRITICAL (Phase 1, 0-30 days). The BCP references third-party vendors (AT&T, RingCentral, AWS, Microsoft 365, Synology) but has no service-provider oversight framework. Add a Service Provider Management section: vendor due-diligence procedures, contractual 72-hour breach-notification requirements, ongoing monitoring of provider security posture, and an inventory of all providers with access to customer information.

Title: Implement Reg S-P Recordkeeping Requirements
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). No recordkeeping provisions for Reg S-P compliance. Define the records to maintain (policies, incident reports, notifications, testing results), the five-year retention period, storage location and format, and the designated records custodian.

Title: Expand Data Backup and Recovery Specifications
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). Backup descriptions are vague (Synology NAS, cloud facilities) and lack specifics. Expand to specify backup frequency and schedule, RPO and RTO per data category, encryption standards at rest and in transit, geographic separation of backup sites, hard-copy document backup procedures, and regular restoration testing with documented results.

Title: Build Mission Critical Systems Inventory
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). The BCP does not identify mission-critical systems. Create an inventory mapping each system to its business function, owner, dependencies, and RTO/RPO, with alternate processing capabilities, covering order management, trade execution, clearing/settlement, customer account access, and fund/securities delivery.

Title: Develop Financial and Operational Assessment Procedures
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). No written procedures for financial and operational assessment following a significant business disruption. Add procedures to assess the firm's ability to fund operations, evaluate changes to credit-risk exposure, determine ability to meet margin/capital requirements, and establish criteria for continuing or suspending operations.

Title: Document Customer Access to Funds and Securities Procedures
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). The BCP lacks specific procedures for prompt customer access to funds and securities if the firm cannot continue business. Add procedures for transferring or making accounts accessible, arrangements with carrying/clearing firms, timelines for restoring access, and steps if the firm cannot continue (including SIPC notification).

Title: Define Alternate Customer Communication Procedures
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). Customer communication procedures are vague. Define specific alternate channels for each disruption scenario (firm-only, building, district, city, regional): alternate phone numbers, backup email, website notifications, social media, and written correspondence procedures.

Title: Expand Employee Communication and Notification Plan
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). Employee communication relies on a basic call tree. Add a mass notification system, backup communication tools (SMS, messaging apps), out-of-band procedures, and regular call-tree testing with documented results.

Title: Document Critical Business Constituent Impact Procedures
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). No procedures for assessing impact on critical business constituents, banks, and counterparties. Identify all clearing firms, custodians, banks, and counterparties, and add procedures to notify them of disruptions, maintain operational connectivity, access backup systems, and document impact assessments.

Title: Add Regulatory Reporting Continuity Procedures
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). The BCP lacks procedures for maintaining regulatory reporting (FOCUS reports, trade reporting) during a disruption. Add a list of all filings and deadlines, alternate submission methods, designated personnel, and procedures for requesting extensions.

Title: Create BCP Customer Disclosure Statement
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). No customer disclosure provisions. Create a BCP disclosure statement and procedures to provide it at account opening, post it on the firm website, mail it on request, update on material change, and address varying disruption scenarios (firm-only, building, district, city, regional).

Title: Register and Maintain FINRA Contact System Emergency Contacts
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). The BCP lists two emergency contacts but does not reference the FINRA Contact System (FCS) or the update/review requirement. Add procedures to register and maintain emergency contacts in FCS, update promptly on material change, and review annually by the 17th business day after year-end.

Title: Add Supervisory Continuity Provisions
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 2, 30-60 days). The BCP does not address continuity of supervisory functions and Written Supervisory Procedures. Add a Supervisory Continuity section: alternate supervisory personnel, communications review during disruption, customer-account monitoring continuity, trade supervision at alternate locations, and documentation of supervisory activities.

Title: Add Registered Principal Approval and Version Control
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). The BCP shows no registered-principal approval, signature block, approval date, or annual-review evidence. Add a formal approval section with a registered-principal signature block, approval date, annual-review certification, and a version-control/change log.

Title: Expand Cybersecurity Incident Response Integration
Detailed Description: HIGH (Phase 1, 0-30 days). The Unauthorized Access subsection is two vague bullets. Significantly expand the cybersecurity section or reference a standalone Cybersecurity Incident Response Plan covering intrusion detection/prevention, endpoint protection, MFA, network segmentation, forensic investigation, law-enforcement coordination, and cyber insurance.
Source Document: SEC Reg S-P / FINRA Best Practices / Finding 17

Title: Add Customer Information Disposal Procedures
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). No procedures for proper disposal of customer and consumer-report information as required by Reg S-P. Add disposal procedures: secure wiping/degaussing of electronic records, cross-cut shredding of hard copy, disposal documentation, and third-party disposal-vendor oversight.

Title: Comprehensive BCP Refresh and Currency Update
Detailed Description: MEDIUM (Phase 3, 60-90 days). The BCP is over two years old (dated October 2023) with stale personnel, contacts, and technology references. Verify all personnel/contacts/titles, update technology references, document the annual review with date and reviewer, and implement version control.

Title: Document Alternate Site Capabilities and Agreements
Detailed Description: LOW (best practice). The alternate site (84 Spacial, Irvine, CA 92618) and a hotel-conference-room option lack detail. Document available infrastructure (desks, connectivity, phones), capacity, pre-arranged agreements, activation time, and regular readiness testing.

Title: Annual BCP Review and Registered Principal Approval
Detailed Description: Conduct the annual review and update of AFFG's Business Continuity Plan and obtain registered-principal approval. Validate RTO/RPO, recovery strategies, contacts, and technology references; document the review date and reviewer; update the version/change log.

Title: Annual FINRA Contact System Emergency Contact Review
Detailed Description: Review and update AFFG's emergency contacts in the FINRA Contact System (FCS) by the 17th business day following year-end, and update promptly upon any material change. Document each update and the annual review.

Title: Annual BCP Test and Tabletop Exercise
Detailed Description: Plan and execute an annual BCP test (tabletop or functional) across disruption scenarios (firm-only, building, district, city, regional). Validate activation, communications, mission-critical system recovery, and customer-access procedures. Document results and remediation actions.

Title: Quarterly Backup Restoration Testing
Detailed Description: Perform restoration tests of AFFG backups against documented RPO/RTO for each data category. Verify integrity of restored data and document results; remediate failures and retest.

Title: Semi-Annual Employee Call Tree and Notification Test
Detailed Description: Test AFFG's employee emergency notification chain (call tree, mass notification, SMS/messaging backups). Confirm all personnel are reached within target time; document results and update contacts.

Title: Annual Service Provider Oversight Review
Detailed Description: Review all AFFG service providers with access to customer information: confirm contractual 72-hour breach-notification clauses, refresh due-diligence and security-posture assessments, and reconcile the provider inventory. Document findings and required remediation.

Title: Maintain Reg S-P Compliance Records (5-Year Retention)
Detailed Description: Maintain AFFG's Reg S-P records: policies and procedures, incident reports, breach notifications, and testing results, retained for five years in the designated repository. Verify completeness and retention on an ongoing basis.

Title: Maintain Customer BCP Disclosure (Website and Account Opening)
Detailed Description: Keep AFFG's BCP customer disclosure current and available: posted on the firm website, provided at account opening, mailed upon request, and updated on material change.

Title: Annual Reg S-P Incident Response Tabletop
Detailed Description: Exercise AFFG's incident response and customer breach-notification procedures annually via a tabletop simulating unauthorized access to customer information. Validate the 30-day notification timeline and service-provider coordination; document results and improvements.
